paragraphs.zh: 最终，初代欧洲农民还是找到了解 决办法。 这与对夜空的紧密关注有关。 通过每天晚上仔细观察星空，他们 了解到一年中星空的变化，并且能 够预测季节更替。 他们能够计算出精确的日历。 通过密切关注季节的变化， 他们学会了如何种植和收割庄稼， 以便在第一次霜冻前使作物获得 足够的生长时间。 他们还开始驯养奶牛，并学会了用 牛来拉动一项重要的新发明：犁。

paragraphs.en: to pull a critical new invention: the plow. This allowed them to break up the thicker soils of northern Europe and farm new types of land. Now, around 5000 BC, farming began to spread rapidly across Europe. Using simple stone axes and fire, these first farmers began to cut down the trees and turn the forests into farmland. These first farmers all lived together in long communal houses that probably looked a lot like this. Here is another reconstruction of what one of these longhouses might have looked like. These people are develpoed a distinctive style of prttery,which is
paragraphs.zh: 这使得他们可以松动北欧厚实的 土壤，开发全新的土地。 于是，大约在公元前 5000 年，农 业开始在欧洲迅速传播开来。 使用简单的石斧和火，这些初代农 民开始砍伐树木，把森林变成农 田。 他们都住在长形的公共住宅内，看 起来就像这样。 这是另一种长屋原貌的复原。 他们还发展出一种独特的陶器风 格，在这些长屋遗址中也发现了这

paragraphs.en: found in the same sites as the remains of these longhouses. This culture of the First Farmers of Europe has a few names: Danubian Culture, the Longhouse People, or Linear Pottery Culture. Another thing that began to happen was that these first settled Europeans began erecting large stone circles. These are called "megaliths" and they start appearing all across Europe around this time. These megaliths often align with the position of the sun during certain time of year, such as the Summer solstice or Winter solstice. Perhaps special rituals were conducted on those days. These first farmers would also place a horizontal rock on top of several
paragraphs.zh: 些陶器。 这些初代欧洲农民的文化有若干 名称：多瑙河文明，长屋人，以及 线纹陶器文化。 另外，这些最初定居的欧洲人开始 建造大型石圈。 这些被称为“巨石”的石圈开始出 现在欧洲各处。 这些巨石通常与一年中特定时间 的太阳位置保持一致，例如夏至或 冬至。 也许在那些日子里，会有一些特殊 的仪式。 他们还会在几块与地面垂直的岩 石上水平放置一块石头。创造出这

paragraphs.en: vertical rocks.and create a structure called a "Dolmen". Sometimes the Dolmen would then be covered with soil to create a structure called a "Tumulus" . Sometimes a passage would be left open as the Tumulus was created. This created something called a "passage tomb" . You can find these passage tombs scattered all across Europe. Here is another example of one. You would make your way down a passage into the dolmen at the center of the tumulus. This is what the passage tomb looks like from the inside. The bones of the community's ancestors were kept in the center of
paragraphs.zh: 种名为“都尔门”的石棚结构。 有时，他们会在都尔门上覆盖泥 土，形成古墓结构。 有时古墓前还会留有一段通道，这 种墓穴被称为“通道墓”。 这样的墓穴遍布欧洲。这是另一个 例子。 你可以沿着通道，走进位于古墓中 心的都尔门。 从内部看，通道墓是这样的。 生活在这片社区的先人骸骨都被 保存在墓穴中。

paragraphs.en: the tomb. When you died, your body was left there among the bones of your ancestors, and you too became an ancestor. Like the megaliths, some of these passage tombs were designed to line up with the rising or setting sun at certain time of year. On those days, for a few minutes each day, a ray of sunlight would shine down the passage into the heart of the tomb. The central dolmen where all the bones were kept would be filled with warm sunlight. Perhaps special rituals and prayers were said inside the tomb on those days. Of course, to build something like
paragraphs.zh: 当你死后，你的尸骨和祖先放在一 起，而你也成为了一位先人。 和巨石一样，这些通道墓被设计成 在每年特定的时间，与朝阳或落日 连成一线。 在那些日子里，每天都有几分钟， 一缕阳光会照进墓穴的中心。 保存了所有遗骨的都尔门将会被 温暖的阳光所笼罩。 也许在那些日子里，墓穴中会进行 特殊的祈祷和仪式。 当然，要想建造这样的东西，你必

paragraphs.en: this, you have to be very aware of the changing seasons and the position of the sun in the sky during different times of year. The alignment of these megalithic structures with the summer and winter solstices shows a keen awareness of the calendar. Perhaps this is connected to the new focus on the seasons required by farming in Europe. Another interesting thing to notice is that these people lived together in life in longhouses, and their bones were together in death in the passage tombs. This is more evidence that these first European Farmers were people who lived strongly communal lives: the individual was less important than the group.
paragraphs.zh: 须非常清楚季节更替，以及一年中 不同的时间太阳所处的位置。 这些巨石的排列和夏至、冬至时令 的紧密结合，显示了前人对天文历 法的精准认知。 也许这和欧洲农业对季节的要求 这一新焦点有关。 另一件有趣的事是，这些人生前共 同生活在长屋中，死后尸骨还一起 埋在通道墓中。 这些证据表明，初代欧洲农民过着 紧密的集体生活：集体高于个人。

paragraphs.en: So lets go back and look at the big picture. From 6000-5000 BC, farming in Europe was limited to the Mediterranean coast and the Danube river valley. But a new awareness of the calendar and the invention of the plow allowed farming to expand rapidly from 5000 to 4000 BC. Around 5000 BC, just as farming was spreading into every corner of Europe, people in Southeastern Europe and the Middle East were making another discovery which would change the world. So please join me next time as we talk about this discovery, which finally brought the stone age to a close. Thank you all, very much!
paragraphs.zh: 让我们回过头来看看这张大图。 公元前 6000-5000 年，欧洲的农业 被限制在地中海沿岸和多瑙河流 域。 但是，对历法的新认识和犁的发明 使得农业在公元前 5000-4000 年 间迅速扩张。 大约在公元前 5000 年，就在农业 向欧洲的每一个角落蔓延的时候， 欧洲东南部和中东的人们正在进 行另一项探索，这将改变世界。 请加入我的下一次课程，让我们共 同讨论这项结束了石器时代的发 现。 谢谢大家。

paragraphs.en: 19：The Lecture End Hello everyone and welcome back to our series on world history. Today we will discuss a major change in human history: the end of the stone age. And since this lecture series has been mostly about the prehistoric stone age, this also marks the end of this series. We will have one more lecture after this, but that is more of a review or perspective-taking lesson. This is the end of the new material. The stone age is the longest age in human history. In fact in pre-dates modern humans like us. The stone age began 2.5 million years ago, all the way back to Homo
paragraphs.zh: 大家好！欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列讲座。 今天我们将讨论人类历史上一个 重大变化：石器时代的终结。 由于这个系列讲座主讲史前石器 时代，本讲也标志着这个系列的结 束。 在此之后我们将再进行一次讲座， 但那将更像是一次复习或我们之 间的观点交流。 石器时代是人类历史上最长的时 期，事实上，现代人和我们很像。 石器时代开始于 250 万年前，可追 溯到智人时期，我们在第 4 讲曾经

paragraphs.en: habilis. We talked about him in lesson 4 if you remember. Over time we saw a progression in stone-age technology from Mode 1 tools that were just a broken rock, to Mode 2 Hand-axes, all the way to the very advanced polished stone tools of the Neolithic era. But around 5000 BC, just as farming was spreading to the Europe, which is also about 7000 years ago, humans discovered a brand new way of making tools. This discovery probably happened in at least 2 places. Once in southern Europe near a place we now call "Plotchnik" and once in the Middle East near a place we now call "Tell Saf". People living in those areas were drawn to a beautiful green-colored
paragraphs.zh: 探讨过相关内容。 随着时间的推移，我们看到从初代 工具——一块破碎的岩石，到二代 工具——手斧，一直再到新石器时 代——抛光石器，石器时代的技术 在不断进步。 但是在公元前 5000 年，或者说 7000 年前，人类发现了一种全新 的制作工具的方法。 这一发现至少发生在两处：一是在 欧洲南部，我们现在称为“普契尼” 的地方，另一处在中东，我们现在 叫做“塔尔沙夫”。 住在这些地方的人们被一种漂亮 的绿色岩石所吸引，他们可能已经

paragraphs.en: stone.And they probably began to experiment with it. This stone is called "Malachite." Chemically speaking, it is a compound of Copper, Carbon, Oxygen and Hydrogen. This kind of rock is called an "ore", because it contains metal in it, Malachite is a copper-ore. And around 5000 BC, we know that people began to mine this rock in large quantities. First they simply broke off pieces where the rock seen protrudes at the surface. Then they followed these seams into the earth, creating the world's first mines. This picture shows you a copper
paragraphs.zh: 开始实践了。 这种岩石叫做“孔雀石”。 用化学角度来讲，这是一种由铜， 碳，氧和氢组成的化合物。 这种岩石被称为“矿石”，孔雀石 是铜矿石。 我们知道，大约在公元前 5000 年， 人们开始大量采掘这种岩石。 一开始，他们只是采掘裸露在地表 的部分。 然后他们顺着这些矿层向下挖，也 由此产生了世界上最早的矿洞。 这张照片展示了一个 7000 年前被

paragraphs.en: mine that was created around that time 7000 years ago. These miners then shipped these malachite rocks great distances back to towns where they could trade them for all sorts of other things. In the towns, some people had learned something important about this special rock. First you need to grind it down into a powder. Then you can put the powder into a clay bowl and heat it in a fire. If you blow air into the fire with blow pipes you can make that fire very hot. Eventually the powdered malachite will melt into a liquid, and you can pour that liquid into a mold and create pure copper.
paragraphs.zh: 开采的铜矿洞。 然后他们经过长途运输将这些孔 雀石运回城镇，在那里可以交易各 种各样的东西。 在城镇里，有些人已经了解到关于 这些特殊石头的重要的知识。 首先你要将它磨成粉末。 然后你可以把粉末放进一个粘土 碗里并在火上加热。 如果用吹管 将空气吹入火中的话，可以大大提 高其温度。 最终，粉状孔雀石会熔化成液体， 这时可以将该液体倒入模具并制 造纯铜。

paragraphs.en: Human had discovered how to make copper, the world's first manmade metal. This picture I have here is the oldest man-made metal object that we've ever discovered. It is an awl, a tool used for punching holes in fabric, and it was discovered at a location called Tal Saf. It is about 7,000 years old. This discovery marks the end of the stone age and the beginning of the copper age. Over time people learned how to make more sophisticated shapes out of copper. They discovered that you could make a shape out of wax, and then cover the wax with plaster. Then you could pour molten copper
paragraphs.zh: 如此，人类已经学会如何制铜—— 世界上第一种人类可以冶炼出的 金属。 这张图片是我们发现的最古老的 人造金属器物。 它是一种锥子， 是一种用于在织物上打孔的工具， 出土于塔尔沙夫。 它有着约 7000 年的历史，这一发 现标志着石器时代的终结与铜器 时代的开始。 随着时间的推移，人们学会了如何 用铜来造出复杂的形状。 他们发现可以用蜡来塑形，然后再 在其表面覆上一层石膏。 然后就可以把熔化的铜从模具顶

paragraphs.en: into the top of the mold, the wax would burn-off and the copper would fill the shape left behind. Once the copper had cooled, you would be left with a piece of copper made into any shape you wanted. The year 4000 BC was 1000 years into the copper age, and around that time, a town called "Teley-at-al-Rasul" had become one of the most important urban areas in what is now Israel. The Rassulians would trade for copper that was far away, in places like Wadi Faynan. And with this copper they made beautiful works of art. In 1961, an archaeologist in Israel discovered a collection of Rassulian
paragraphs.zh: 端注入，蜡燃尽后，铜就可以填充 模具内空余空间。 液体铜冷却凝固后，你就可以得到 你想要的任何形状的铜器了。 公元前 4000 年是进入铜器时代后 的 1000 年，大约在那个时候，拉 索尔，一个位于如今以色列的小 镇，已经成为当时最重要的城市之 一。 拉索尔人可以将铜器贩到远至费 南河谷的地方。 用铜，他们制作出精美的工艺品。 1961 年，以色列的一位考古学家 发现了一堆 6000 年前被埋在洞穴

paragraphs.en: copper objects that had been buried in a cave 6000 years ago, and then never retrieved. This collection is know as the Nahal Mishmar horde, and it shows us just how sophisticated and skilled the Rassulians became at making beautiful copper objects. The town of Rassul has been an important archaeological site for decades. In addition to the copper objects, we've also found beautiful artwork on the inside walls of the town's building. One of the most famous is this is the star of Rassul. The Rassulians also made artwork and tools out of pottery, as you can see from this picture.
paragraphs.zh: 中的拉索尔铜制器物，然后再也没 有找到类似的发现。 这些文物被称为纳哈尔-米沙马 群，它向我们展示了拉索尔人在制 作美丽的铜制品方面拥有极其复 杂和熟练的技术。 几十年来，拉索尔镇一直是重要的 考古遗址。 除了铜制品外，我们还在镇上的建 筑内墙上找到了精美的艺术品。 其中最著名的就是拉索尔之星。 如图所示，拉索尔人还用陶制作了 陶器艺术品和工具。

paragraphs.en: Rassul is the "representative site" for this period in middle eastern history. It shows us the sophistication of copper-age socieities. But the copper age did not just stay limited to where it began. Like farming, the knowledge of and use of copper spread outwards from its places of discovery. And so by 4000 BC, Europe and the Middle East had left the stone age behind and entered the CopperAge, a period of 2000 years long in human history. The copper age ended with the discovery of Bronze around 3000 BC. This isALSO the same time that people first invented writing. And with writing we can begin
paragraphs.zh: 拉索尔是这个时期中东历史上的 “标志性代表”。它向我们展示了 铜器时代社会的复杂性。 但是铜器时代并不仅仅拘泥于其 发源的一隅。 与农业一样，关于铜的了解和使用 从发源地向外传播开来。 于是到了公元前 4000 年，欧洲和 中东已经由石器时代进入到了延 续 2000 年的铜器时代。 铜器时代随着约公元前 3000 年青 铜的发现而终结，同时，这也是人 类最早发明文字的时代。 文字，使我们脱离了史前时代，开

paragraphs.en: proper history and leave prehistory behind. So with the discovery of copper and the end of the stone age, this lecture series on human history must also draw to a close. The next chapter in the story is the discovery of bronze, the discovery of writing, the first cities and the first advanced civilizations of Egypt and Sumer. But that my friends, is a story for another time. Thank you very much!
paragraphs.zh: 始了真正意义上的历史。 随着铜的发现和石器时代的终结， 我们关于史前人类的系列讲座也 将暂告一段落。 下一阶段将是青铜的发现、文字的 发明，最早的城市和最早的埃及同 苏美尔的先进文明。 但是朋友们，那又将是另一段故事 啦！ 谢谢大家！

titleZh: 第二十讲：原来今年不是 2018 年，而是人类纪年 12018 年！

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone. As we reach the end of the stone age, and the end of our first unit on world history, I think it is a good time to pause and reflect. The goal of this course, and of any good history class, is to better understand where we fit in the big story of the Human Race. To think about our place a little better I want to talk to you about time, and about how we are living in a special time in the history of our planet. Before I began to teach history, my knowledge of some historical periods was a little hazy. For example I knew that the
paragraphs.zh: 年，而是人类纪年 12018 年！ 大家好。 当我们抵达石器时代的终点，来 到世界历史系列课程第一单元结 课之际，我认为这是一个暂停和 反思的好时机。 这门课程，以及任何优秀的历史 课程，其共同目标都是为了帮助 我们更好地理解自身在人类历史 进程中所处的位置。 想要更好地思考我们的位置，我 想和你们谈谈时间，以及我们是 如何生活在这个星球历史上一个 特殊的时期。 在我开始教授历史之前，我对一 些历史时期的了解是比较模糊 的。 举个例子，我知道农业和第一个

paragraphs.en: discovery of farming and the first cities both happened thousands of years ago, and they both happened first in the Middle East, specifically in the Fertile Crescent. I could also have told you that the first cities were in Sumer, in Mesopotamia. But I didn't have the exact dates very clear in my head. I was more interested in Roman history or modern history, and didn't know as much about the very beginning of civilization. So both the invention of farming and the first cities fell into that hazy category of 'a long time ago' in my mind. If you had asked me, I would have known that farming happened first, because farms are necessary for
paragraphs.zh: 城市的发现都发生在几千年前， 它们都发生在中东的新月沃土地 带。 我可能也告诉过你，最早的一批 城市出现在美索不达米亚平原的 苏美尔地区。 但我并不知道明确的日期。我对 罗马历史或现代历史更感兴趣， 却不太了解文明的开端。 因此，在我看来，农业和第一批 城市的出现都属于“很久以前” 的模糊范畴。 如果你问我，我就会知道农业出 现得更早，因为农场是城市的必 需品。

paragraphs.en: cities. But I would have told you that the first farms and the first civilizations probably happened around the same time. Maybe the first farms were a few centuries before the first cities. But certainly not much more than that. But when I looked up the numbers, I learned that my intuition was waaaay off. The first farms began 12,000 years ago. That's 6,000 years before ancient Sumer and ancient Egypt. To put it another way. We are about as long after ancient Sumer as ancient Sumer was after the first Natufian farmers.
paragraphs.zh: 但我想告诉你们的是，第一批农 场和第一批文明可能发生在同一 时间。 也许第一批农场比第一批城市早 出现几个世纪。但也不可能早更 多了。 但当我查阅史料，我发现我的直 觉出现了重大错误。 第一批农场开始于 12,000 年前。 这比古代苏美尔人和古埃及人早 了 6000 年。 换句话说。 我们和古苏美尔人的时间距离， 几乎与后者和纳图夫农民之间一 样遥远。

paragraphs.en: Here's another one of those history problems. In the minds of most people, the Pyramids are really old, and the Roman Empire is also really old. So we put both things into the mental category of 'A long time ago.' But the Roman Empire happened 2000 years ago. The Pyramids were built 4000 years ago. In other words, today we are as far from the Romans empire as the Romans were from the building of the Pyramids. This kind of problem can also happen with famous historical figures. Here are two people that I'm pretty sure that you've heard of: Buddha
paragraphs.zh: 还有另一个问题。 在大多数人的心目中，金字塔真 的很古老，罗马帝国也很古老。 所以我们把这两件事都放到了 “很久以前”的心理范畴。 但罗马帝国是 2000 年前的事。金 字塔是在 4000 年前建造的。 换句话说，如今我们与罗马人的 时间距离，如同后者与金字塔一 样遥远。 这类问题也可能出现在著名的历 史人物身上。 这两个人，我很确定你们听说过： 佛祖和孔子。

paragraphs.en: and Confucius. Now take one moment and think, don't look it up. Which man was born first? How many years apart did the live? Did they live thousands of years apart, hundreds of years apart? What century did each man live in? Just make a guess in your mind right now. I will give you the answer in half a minute. Pause the video if you need to think about it. Okay did you make a guess? They lived 12 years apart. The historical Buddha is the Indian prince Siddhartha Gautama. He was born in 563 BC. Confucius was born in 551 BC. In other words, the Buddha was just
paragraphs.zh: 现在，花一点时间想想。不要查 资料。 谁更早出生？他们生活的年代相 隔多久？他们是相隔数千年，还 是数百年？ 他们分别生活在哪个世纪？ 现在就猜一猜。我将在半分钟后 给你答案。 好了，你们猜到了吗？ 他们相隔 12 年。 历史上的佛祖是印度王子悉达 多·乔答摩。他出生于公元前 563 年。 孔子出生于公元前 551 年。 换句话说，佛祖只比孔子年长 12

paragraphs.en: 12 years older than Confucius. Their difference in ages was similar to the difference between an older brother and a younger brother，or the difference between me and a college student. Part of the problem is that our current calendar was originally based upon the birth of Jesus Christ, which happens in the year 1. The problem is this system can be confusing because 1 AD is the year right after 1 BC. There is no year 0. This means you can sometimes miss a year when you are trying to calculate dates in BC. Also its tricky to count backwards. So this Calendar tends to de-emphasize everything that happened more than 2018 years
paragraphs.zh: 岁。 他们的年龄差宛如兄弟,或者宛 如我和一个大学生。 部分问题在于，我们当前的历法 最初是基于耶稣基督的诞生，而 这发生在公元 1 年。 问题是，这个体系可能会让人困 惑，因为公元 1 年是公元前 1 年 之后的一年。没有第 0 年。 也就是说，当你在计算公元前的 年份时，很有可能会漏算一年。 同样，向后数也很困难。 因此，这种日历算法往往会弱化 公元前发生的事情。

paragraphs.en: ago. Also when you start thinking about events in the Neolithic you have to remember to add 2018 years. I kept forgetting that 10,000 BC is actually 12,000 years ago. Now our current calendar actually works pretty well for the most part, because the history that really affects our lives mainly happened in last few hundred years. But when you start thinking about the Neolithic, then our calendar can be a little confusing, and distort our sense of the past. So in 1993, the Italian scientist Cesare Emiliani proposed adopting a new calendar. If you remember the 8th lecture, I talked about Gobekli Tepe.
paragraphs.zh: 当你开始思考新石器时代的事件 时，你必须记住要加上 2018 年。 我就总是忘记公元前 10,000 年实 际上是 12,000 年前。 现在，我们的历法在大多数情况 下都能正常使用，是因为对我们 生活有重大影响的事件往往发生 在最近数百年中。 但是当你开始思考新石器时代的 时候，我们的历法可能会有点混 乱，扭曲我们对过去的感觉。 因此在 1993 年，意大利科学家切 萨雷·埃米利亚尼提议采用一个 新的历法。 如果你们还记得第八课的内容， 我们讨论过哥贝克利遗址。

paragraphs.en: The very first large building project in human history. Here is a picture of it to remind you. Now Gobekli Tepe was built in several phases. But it was mainly built around the year 10,000 BC. 10,000 BC was also the time that the Natufians first began to farm. The first farming and the first building projects also roughly correspond to the end of the Younger Dryas Ice Age. In a way, we could think of 10,000 BC as the point when we humans started to become the most powerful force on the planet. Our buildings, cities, and farms
paragraphs.zh: 人类历史上第一个大型建筑工 程。 请看这张图片，以便大家回忆。 哥贝克利石阵的建造分几个阶 段。 但它是在公元前 10,000 年前后建 成的。 公元前 10,000 年也是纳图人第一 次开始耕种的时候。 最初农业与建筑的出现也大致相 当于新仙女木时代的结束。 在某种程度上，我们可以把公元 前一万年看作是我们人类开始成 为地球上最强力量的时候。 我们的建筑、城市和农场将改变

paragraphs.en: would transform the face of the Earth. But this date also comes before every human civilization. So a calendar based on 10,000 BC would be fair to all cultures. Therefore Emiliani proposed that the year 10,001 BC be declared 'Year 0' of the Human Era. He called this new calendar the 'Holocene Calendar.' 'Holocene' is a geology term that means 'Entirely Recent' and it refers to the period since the last ice age, which is also started roughly about 10,000 BC. The great thing about the New Calendar, is the only thing you have to do to adopt it is just put a '1' in front of the year. Days, Months, and
paragraphs.zh: 地球的面貌。 而这个日期，也早于所有人类文 明。因此，以公元前 10,000 年为 起点的历法，对所有文明都是公 平的。 因此，埃米利亚尼提出，将公元 前 10001 年作为人类时代的“第 0 年”。 他称这个新历法为“全新世历”。 “全新世”是一个地质学术语， 意思是“完全近代”，它指的是 自上个冰河世纪以来的一段时 期，也就是大约在公元前 10,000 年左右开始的。 新日历的伟大之处在于，你唯一 需要做的一件事，就是在年份前 加上一个“1”。日、月、节日， 都不受影响。

paragraphs.en: Holidays can all stay exactly the same. So instead of living in the year 2018, we are actually living in the year 12,018. Now as a mental exercise, let's explore some major historical events using the new calendar. I have put the Human Era date at the top of each slide. But to help you keep track, I've also included the traditional date in the top right corner. In the year 0 HE, Gobekli Tepe was being built. In the year 0, the Natufians were starting the very first farms. By 500 HE, we see the v ery first agricultural villages like Jericho andJeff-al-ahkmar.
paragraphs.zh: 所以我们不是活在 2018 年，而是 活在 12018 年。 现在，作为一种脑力锻炼，让我 们用新历法来探索重大的历史事 件。 我把人类时代的日期写在每张幻 灯片的顶部。但是为了辅助理解， 我还把传统的日期写在右上角。 在全新世历 0 年，哥贝克利石阵 建成了。 全新世历 0 年，纳图夫人开始在 第一批农场劳作。 到全新世历 500 年，我们看到了 第一批农业村庄，例如杰利科和 杰夫艾哈迈尔。

paragraphs.en: By 1000 HE, Jericho has grown into a sizable town. From 1000 - 3000 HE, we see the oldest Neolithic communities in China like Nanzhuangtou, Pengtoushan and Peiligang. In 2000 HE, Jericho builds the first wall and tower. The statues of 'Ain Ghazal were built around 3000 HE. Chatal Hayak also began around 3000 HE. In 3000 HE, the Native Americans of Mexico also developed agriculture. In America their staple crop was corn instead of wheat. By 4000 HE the Middle Easterners were bringing the Idea of farming into Europe.
paragraphs.zh: 到全新世历 1000 年，杰利科已经 成为一个相当大的城镇 全新世历 1000-3000 年间，我们 可以看到中国最古老的新石器时 代社区，像南庄头，彭头山和裴 李岗。 全新世历 2000 年，杰利科有了第 一座城墙和第一座塔。 艾因加扎勒雕像是在全新世历 3000 年左右建造的。 加泰土丘也开始于全新世历 3000 年左右。 全新世历 3000 年，墨西哥的印第 安人也发展了农业。在美洲，他 们的主要作物是玉米而不是小 麦。 到全新世历 4000 年，中东人将农 业的理念引入了欧洲。

paragraphs.en: They were intermarrying with the local Europeans and sharing knowledge. One of the most famous sites where this happened was Lepenski Vir in Serbia. 500 years later, scattered farms had begun to spread around Europe. At this point the Europeans were living in communal longhouse. We call them the 'Longhouse people' And then around 5000 HE, humans discovered how to make copper. This event marks the end of the stone age. The wheel was first invented in 5500 HE. First it was used to make pottery. Later it was used to build carts and then chariots.
paragraphs.zh: 他们与当地的欧洲人通婚，分享 知识。其中一个最著名的地点是 塞尔维亚的莱潘斯基维尔。 500 年后，分散的农场开始遍布欧 洲各处。 这时欧洲人住在公共的长屋中。 我们称他们为“长屋人”。 然后，大约在全新世历 5000 年， 人类发现了如何制铜。 这一事件标志着石器时代的结 束。 轮子是在全新世历 5500 年发明 的。最初它被用来制作陶器。后 来，它被用来建造马车和战车。

paragraphs.en: About 1000 years later, in 6400 HE, the very first writing began. The invention of writing marks the end of 'prehistory' and the beginning of 'history.' About the same time, people living in Central Asia were domesticating the horse. And then in 6700 HE, we finally see the very first cities emerge in ancient Sumer. If 'civilization' means that you have both writing and cities, then Sumer was the first civilization. But Egyptian civilization also emerges at around the same time. And several hundred miles away in the Indus River valley, another great civilization was emerging.
paragraphs.zh: 大约 1000 年后，在全新世历 6400 年，第一次书写开始了。 书写的发明标志着“史前”的终 结和“历史”的开始。 大约在同一时间，生活在中亚的 人们正在驯养马匹。 然后在全新世历 6700 年，我们终 于看到了第一批城市出现在古代 苏美尔。 如果“文明”意味着同时拥有书 写和城市，那么苏美尔是最初的 文明。 但埃及文明也几乎在同一时间出 现。 在几百英里之外的印度河流域， 另一个伟大的文明正在崛起。

paragraphs.en: At about this time, people learned how to add tin or arsenic to the copper and make bronze. This moment begins the 'bronze age.' We can see that it starts about 1700 years after the 'copper age.' The Egyptians begin to build their Pyramids about 600 years into the Bronze Age. While the Egyptians are building the Pyramids the British are building Stone-Henge. So the two monuments are about the same age. Then 400 years after Stone-Henge, the Xia Dynasty begins in China. As you know, traditional Chinese history begins with the Xia, which is 1300 years younger than Sumer or Egypt.
paragraphs.zh: 大约在这个时候，人们学会了如 何在铜中加入锡或砷，来制造青 铜。 这一刻，“青铜时代”开始了。 我们可以看到，它开始于“铜时 代”之后的 1700 年。 进入青铜时代 600 年后，埃及人 开始建造金字塔。 埃及人在建造金字塔时，英国人 正在建造巨石阵。 因此，这两处遗迹的时间相仿。 巨石阵出现 400 年后，中国进入 夏朝。 如你所知，中国的传统历史始于 夏，比苏美尔或埃及年轻了 1300 岁。

paragraphs.en: BUT people were farming in this part of the world for 7000 years BEFORE any of the dynasties. That means there is a HUGE amount of Chinese history that is unappreciated by most Chinese people. We need to be doing A LOT more research into the Chinese Neolithic Era. China is missing 2/3rds of its history! The Shang dyansty begins 800 years later. 8800 HE is also the age of the oracle bones. Here is where the written record of Chinese history begins. 8800 years after the very first farmers, and 7000 years after the
paragraphs.zh: 但是在这一地区，在任何朝代出 现之前，人们已经耕作了 7000 年。 这就意味着，中国历史中有很大 一部分都不为国人所熟知。我们 需要对中国新石器时代做更多的 研究。 中国的历史有三分之二可供探 索！ 800 年后，商朝开始了。 全新世历 8800 年也是甲骨文的年 代。 这是中国历史的书面记录开始的 时间。 世界上第一批农民出现的 8800 年 后，也是中国第一批农民出现的

paragraphs.en: first farmers in China. At about the same time that the Shang is flourishing in China, the Bronze Age civilizations are collapsing in the Eastern Mediterranean. And at the same time, people discover how to forge iron. Shortly after the Bronze Age Collapse, the Alphabet is invented. So iron and the Alphabet are about the same age. So remember, writing is 2500 years older than the alphabet. If we jump ahead to the 9400s, we reach the lives of Confucius and Buddha. About 200 years later, Alexander the Great conquers the Persian Empire.
paragraphs.zh: 7000 年后。 商朝在中国蓬勃发展的同时，青 铜时代的文明正在东地中海分崩 离析。 与此同时，人们发现了如何锻造 铁。 青铜时代崩溃后不久，字母表就 被发明出来了,他们是大概一个 时代的产物。 记住，书写比字母表早出现 2500 年。 如果我们跳到全新世历 95 世纪， 我们就可以触及到孔子和佛祖的 生活。 大约 200 年后，亚历山大大帝征 服了波斯帝国。

paragraphs.en: This happens around 9645 HE. 135 years later, Qin Shi Huang unifies China in 9780 HE. Julius Cesar is murdered in 9957 HE. Jesus Christ is born in the year 10,000. The Western Roman empire falls in 10,476 HE. The first crusade reaches Jersualem in 11,099 HE. Christopher Columbus arrives in the Americas in 11,492HE. The French Revolution happens in 11,789HE The first atom bomb explodes in 11,945HE.
paragraphs.zh: 这一事件大约发生于全新世历 135 年后，全新世历 9780 年，秦 始皇统一六国。 尤利乌斯·凯撒于全新世历 9957 年被谋杀。 耶稣基督出生于全新世历 10,000 年。 西罗马帝国于全新世历 10,476 年 灭亡。 第一次十字军东征于全新世历 11,099 年抵达耶路撒冷。 哥伦布于全新世历 11,492 年到达 美洲。 法 国大 革 命 发生 在 全 新世 历 第一颗原子弹于全新世历 11,945 年爆炸。

paragraphs.en: The first man walks on the moon in 11,969HE. And today it is 12,018HE. And that brings us to the end of our journey. I hope this little trip through time with Emiliani's Calendar has helped you to see the past in a new way. And who knows what the future will bring. Thank you all very much!
paragraphs.zh: 全新世历 11,969 年，人类第一次 踏上月球。 而今天，是全新世历 12,018 年。 这就把我们带到了旅程的终点。 我希望这趟带着艾米利亚尼历法 的时间之旅能让你以一种全新的 方式来看待过去。 谁知道未来又会带来什么。 谢谢大家！
